crime.value: Missing child - 5-year-old Vincent Craxton has disappeared

victim.value: Vincent Craxton, 5-year-old boy with blonde hair, described as his father's 'whole life'
victim.quote: My son is all I really have, he's my whole life!

investigator.value: Unnamed female narrator, District Attorney assistant with 5 years experience, justice-focused professional
investigator.quote: I worked as an assistant for the District Attorney, Larissa Hardwicke. I've been an assistant for five years

suspect_architecture.value: Three suspects with family dynamics: Samuel Craxton (father, nervous behavior, delayed police report), Mrs. Craxton (stepmother, hostile, deflecting blame), and implicit third party (biological mother referenced but absent)
suspect_architecture.quote: I-I haven't yet. No,' he said, appearing almost nervous... 'What the hell are you doing? Where's my stepson?'

red_herring.value: Father's suspicious delay in calling police and nervous behavior presented as potential guilt indicators
red_herring.quote: I-I haven't yet. No,' he said, appearing almost nervous.

social_world.value: Legal/law enforcement hierarchy with District Attorney office, police departments, and jurisdictional protocols governing missing persons cases
social_world.quote: I worked as an assistant for the District Attorney... I called Missing Persons... went to Special Victims

media_and_audience.value: Amber Alert system activated for nationwide public notification of missing child case
media_and_audience.quote: sent out a nationwide Amber Alert

investigator_wound.value: Narrator's romantic attraction to the suspect father compromises professional judgment and creates conflict of interest
investigator_wound.quote: I was in lust with Sam. Hard lust, a lust that I had never felt before
